hero.title: 9 Days, Shimla & Manali.
hero.titleAccent: The Best of the Himachal Hills.
hero.intro: A full nine days in the Himalayan foothills. The colonial charm of Shimla, the snow peaks and adventures of Manali, the Kullu Valley and the high passes. The complete Himachal hill experience, unhurried.
hero.heroImageAlt: Shimla and Manali Himachal hill stations

overview.heading: 9 Days Shimla & Manali: The Full Picture
body: This 9 day tour covers the two great hill stations of Himachal Pradesh in depth. Shimla, the colonial queen of hill stations, and Manali, the snow-peaked adventure town on the Beas river, with the Kullu Valley, high passes and the famous toy train along the way.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip is for travellers who want the Himachal hills without rushing. Your own vehicle and driver guide link the colonial Mall Road of Shimla, the Solang Valley and Rohtang adventures of Manali, and the scenic valleys between, at a relaxed pace through cool mountain air.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Shimla's Colonial Charm
items.body: The Mall Road, the Ridge, Christ Church and the mock-Tudor heritage of the former summer capital of British India, plus the scenic Kalka-Shimla toy train.

items.title: Manali's Snow & Adventure
items.body: Solang Valley, the Rohtang Pass and Atal Tunnel, hot springs and the Beas river, the snow-peaked heart of Himachal adventure.

items.title: Kullu Valley & High Passes
items.body: The apple orchards and shawl weavers of the Kullu Valley, the dramatic mountain drives, and the high-altitude landscapes between the two hill stations.

whyBook.heading: The Himachal Hills, in Full
whyBook.body: Covering Shimla, Manali and the high passes over nine days takes good routing and an understanding of mountain roads and permits. We handle it all. Dhesu has run North India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Shimla & Manali Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 9 day package is the most complete Himachal hills tour we offer, covering both Shimla and Manali in depth, with the Kullu Valley and high passes between.

A private vehicle and driver guide handle the mountain roads, the toy train and the high-altitude permits, so you can focus on the scenery and the cool mountain air.

For a combination with the Golden Triangle, see our 10 day Golden Triangle plus Manali package.

sections.heading: Why Nine Days for the Himachal Hills
sections.body:
Shorter trips to Himachal have to choose between Shimla and Manali. Nine days lets you experience both properly, plus the scenic Kullu Valley and the dramatic high passes, without rushing.

The relaxed pace suits the mountains, leaving time for adventure activities, hot springs, and simply enjoying the views, the orchards and the pine forests.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chandigarh or Kullu-Manali Airport (IXC / KUU). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for Shimla and Manali?
items.answer: March to June is pleasant and popular, with lingering snow at high points. December to February brings full snow, beautiful but cold. The monsoon (July to September) can cause landslides. October to November offers crisp, clear mountain views.

items.question: Is the Rohtang Pass guaranteed?
items.answer: No. Rohtang Pass is seasonal and requires permits, usually open from around May to October, weather permitting. The Atal Tunnel offers year-round access to the Lahaul valley. We arrange the best available high-altitude excursion for your dates.

items.question: How much driving is involved?
items.answer: This is a mountain tour, so there are several scenic drives, including the journey between Shimla and Manali via the Kullu Valley. The nine-day pace keeps each day comfortable, and the drives themselves are part of the experience.
